source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera
note: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed including 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA: 1,407+ civilian deaths including 214 children. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed, 18,551+ injured, 300+ health/emergency facilities damaged. Iran's official floor: 1,500+ killed. WHO: 18 hospitals struck. Iran UN ambassador: ~10,000 civilian sites struck. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Internet blackout 310+ hrs (~1% connectivity). Day 27: Iran rejects Trump's 15-point plan; protests continue.
Schoolgirls and staff killed in missile strike on primary school. Pentagon preliminary assessment (Mar 12) suggests US at fault; formal investigation opened.

Includes Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei (Feb 28). Ali Larijani (National Security Council Secretary) confirmed killed Mar 17–18 per IRGC. Mojtaba Khamenei status: reportedly wounded, mother/wife/son reportedly killed (unverified). Netanyahu: 'More than half of Iran's most senior leadership' eliminated. Figures are evolving.

13 KIA confirmed. Mar 28: Iran struck Prince Sultan AB (Saudi Arabia) — 10 US service members wounded (2 seriously), refueling aircraft damaged; total wounded now 150+. 13 KIA: 6 Army Reserve (Kuwait drone strike, Feb 28); Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (died Mar 8); 6 KC-135 crew (Iraq crash, Mar 13). ~150 total wounded. USS Gerald Ford 30-hour fire Mar 18 — no confirmed sailor fatalities. Joe Kent (NCTC) resigned Day 19.

source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel
note: 19+ killed in Israel from Iranian/Hezbollah attacks. 4,800+ wounded total. Mar 28 (Day 29): Iranian missile struck Eshtaol (Jerusalem area) — IDF interception failure, several lightly injured; Houthi missile toward Beersheba. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rockets. Mar 24: Iranian missile in Tel Aviv — 3 injured. Mar 18: Elderly couple killed in Ramat Gan. 175+ Iranian attack waves since Feb 28.

source: UAE Ministry of Defense / UAE authorities
note: Mar 18: UAE Shah Gas Field attacked by Iranian drone — operations suspended (no direct casualties confirmed). Mar 17: 1 Palestinian civilian killed by missile in Abu Dhabi; Dubai airport fuel tank struck. Earlier: 2 armed forces officers killed in helicopter crash (Mar 9). 4 killed and 112+ injured from Iranian strikes to Mar 16. Mar 24: Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly 'losing patience' — considering joining coalition strikes on Iran (Bloomberg).

source: Kuwait Ministry of Health / US State Department
note: Mar 17: Iranian ballistic missile struck US Embassy Kuwait — Rubio ordered closure (no US fatalities from embassy strike). Earlier: 2 Kuwaiti killed from Iranian projectiles.

source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
note: 1,142+ killed including 130+ children; 3,315+ injured; 1M+ displaced. Mar 28: IDF struck Beirut Dahiyeh district and south Lebanon overnight; Al Manar and Al Mayadeen journalists killed in strike. Lebanese President Aoun condemned attacks. Mar 25: Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets at IDF positions. IDF 30km security zone to Litani River. ~700 Hezbollah fighters eliminated per IDF.
Qatar intercepted Iranian missile on Mar 17; blasts heard in Doha. Qatar LNG force majeure risk flagged. Qatar FM warned Gulf states face Iranian retaliation if US ends war unilaterally.

Includes 4 Popular Mobilization Forces fighters killed; 3 injured. Mar 18: Drone attack on US Embassy Baghdad (ongoing). Abu Ali al-Askari (Kata'ib Hezbollah security chief) killed in Mar 14 Baghdad airstrike — announced Mar 17.

Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion, 7th Battalion of Chasseurs Alpins (Varces), killed in Iranian drone strike on joint French-Peshmerga base at Mala Qara, Makhmour district (40km from Erbil), March 13. First French combat fatality.

1 Indian crew member killed aboard MV Safesea Vishnu (Basra, Mar 13); 1 crew killed off Oman coast (Mar 1); 1 other in earlier Hormuz attack. 25+ crew rescued in Basra attacks. 16 ships struck in Gulf total. Hormuz traffic nearly doubled Day 19 as Iran allows more ships.
At least 32 injured (including children) from Iranian strike on Sitra island; Bapco refinery struck causing force majeure; 1 fatality from earlier intercepted missile debris.

Two foreign nationals (Indian and Bangladeshi) killed in Iranian projectile strike on Al-Kharj compound. Saudi forces intercepted 37 Iranian drones over Eastern Province (Mar 17) and additional barrages. Aramco infrastructure on high alert.
Fatality and injuries from projectile hitting a tanker off Muscat coast.
Jordan targeted by 119 Iranian missiles and drones since Feb 28. No fatalities reported.
Protesters stormed US Consulate; security forces opened fire.
